seo.title: Fintech Agentic RAG AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for agentic rag AI agents in fintech. Covers SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying Agentic RAG in fintech environments introduces compounded risk: autonomous query planning across multiple financial data sources can produce synthesized outputs that carry regulatory weight under SOX, SEC, and BSA/AML frameworks. Unlike static RAG pipelines, multi-step retrieval agents can silently propagate hallucinated citations or cross-source inconsistencies into outputs that influence credit decisions, transaction monitoring, or investment guidance. This checklist operationalizes the controls required to deploy these systems safely, auditably, and in compliance with critical financial regulations.

sections.heading: Data Governance & Source Integrity
sections.description: Ensure every data source ingested by the retrieval agent meets financial-grade data quality, lineage, and access control standards before it participates in query planning or synthesis.

items.label: Classify and tag all retrieval sources by regulatory sensitivity
items.description: Label each data source (e.g., SEC filings, transaction ledgers, credit bureau feeds) with its regulatory classification (PII, PCI, MNPI) so the agent's query planner can enforce source-level access policies at runtime.

items.label: Enforce TLS 1.3+ for all in-transit retrieval calls
items.description: All HTTP/S calls made by the agent to internal and external data sources must use TLS 1.3 or higher, with certificate pinning for third-party financial data APIs, satisfying PCI DSS Requirement 4.2.1.

items.label: Validate AES-256 encryption at rest for all indexed financial documents
items.description: Confirm that vector stores, document caches, and retrieval indexes containing transaction data, customer PII, or credit information are encrypted at rest using AES-256, with key management via a FIPS 140-2 validated KMS.

items.label: Implement data freshness SLAs per source type
items.description: Define and enforce maximum staleness thresholds for each source (e.g., market data ≤15 minutes, regulatory filings ≤24 hours, customer account data ≤5 minutes) and block agent queries to sources that exceed their SLA.

items.label: Maintain a versioned data lineage log for every ingested document
items.description: Record the ingestion timestamp, source URL or system of record, document hash, and schema version for every document in the retrieval corpus to support SOX audit trails and SEC examination requests.

items.label: Restrict cross-tenant data retrieval with row-level security
items.description: Enforce row-level or document-level security in the vector store so that an agent session scoped to one customer or business unit cannot retrieve documents belonging to another, preventing GDPR cross-contamination.

items.label: Audit third-party financial data provider contracts for AI usage rights
items.description: Verify that data licensing agreements with providers (Bloomberg, Refinitiv, credit bureaus) explicitly permit use of their data as retrieval context for AI-generated outputs, especially outputs that may constitute financial advice.

sections.heading: Agentic Query Planning & Retrieval Controls
sections.description: Govern the autonomous planning behavior of the agent to prevent unsafe query sequences, scope creep across regulated data domains, and retrieval of information that could produce non-compliant synthesized outputs.

items.label: Define and enforce a query planning policy ruleset
items.description: Implement a declarative policy layer (e.g., OPA or a custom guardrail) that evaluates each planned sub-query against allowed data domains, user permissions, and regulatory constraints before execution, blocking non-compliant query plans.

items.label: Cap maximum retrieval hops and sub-query depth per agent session
items.description: Set hard limits on the number of retrieval steps (e.g., max 5 hops) and recursive sub-queries the agent can execute in a single session to prevent runaway retrieval chains that could exfiltrate excessive financial data.

items.label: Log every planned query, executed retrieval, and retrieved chunk with timestamps
items.description: Persist a structured audit log entry for each query planning decision, retrieval call, and returned document chunk, including relevance scores and source identifiers, to satisfy SOX Section 802 record-keeping requirements.

items.label: Implement cross-source consistency validation before synthesis
items.description: Before the agent synthesizes a final response, run an automated consistency check that flags contradictions between retrieved chunks from different sources (e.g., conflicting account balances or regulatory interpretations) and escalates to human review.

items.label: Block retrieval queries that pattern-match to MNPI or insider information indicators
items.description: Deploy a pre-retrieval classifier that detects query patterns associated with material non-public information (e.g., pre-announcement earnings queries, M&A target searches) and blocks or escalates them to compliance officers.

items.label: Validate query planning accuracy against a golden test set quarterly
items.description: Maintain a curated set of 200+ fintech-specific query scenarios with expected retrieval plans and run automated regression tests quarterly to detect query planning drift that could lead to incorrect source selection.

items.label: Implement rate limiting and anomaly detection on retrieval volume per session
items.description: Set per-session retrieval volume thresholds and deploy anomaly detection to flag sessions that retrieve abnormally large volumes of customer or transaction data, which may indicate prompt injection or data exfiltration attempts.

sections.heading: Output Verification, Explainability & Hallucination Controls
sections.description: Ensure every synthesized output from the agent is grounded in retrieved sources, carries required regulatory disclaimers, and can be fully explained for credit decision compliance and SEC examination purposes.

items.label: Run automated hallucination detection on every synthesized response before delivery
items.description: Apply an NLI-based faithfulness scorer (e.g., using a fine-tuned financial domain model) to verify that every factual claim in the synthesized output is entailed by at least one retrieved source chunk, with a minimum faithfulness threshold of 0.85.

items.label: Attach verified inline citations with source metadata to every output
items.description: Require the agent to produce inline citations for every factual claim, including source document ID, page/section reference, retrieval timestamp, and confidence score, enabling auditors to trace any output back to its source within 60 seconds.

items.label: Append mandatory financial advice disclaimers to all investment or credit-related outputs
items.description: Automatically append SEC-compliant disclaimers ('This is not financial advice...') and FINRA-required disclosures to any output classified as investment guidance, credit assessment, or market analysis, regardless of user prompt framing.

items.label: Generate ECOA-compliant adverse action explanations for credit-related decisions
items.description: For any output that influences a credit decision, produce a structured explanation listing the top factors from retrieved data that contributed to the assessment, satisfying Equal Credit Opportunity Act adverse action notice requirements.

items.label: Implement a confidence threshold gate that triggers human review for low-confidence outputs
items.description: Define a minimum aggregate confidence threshold (e.g., 0.75) combining faithfulness score, cross-source consistency score, and citation coverage; route outputs below this threshold to a licensed compliance officer before delivery.

items.label: Store immutable output snapshots with full retrieval context for 7 years
items.description: Persist every agent output alongside its complete retrieval context (all retrieved chunks, query plan, source metadata) in a WORM-compliant storage system for a minimum of 7 years to satisfy SOX and SEC Rule 17a-4 record retention requirements.

items.label: Test citation verification accuracy monthly against a labeled benchmark
items.description: Maintain a labeled dataset of 500+ fintech outputs with ground-truth citation correctness labels and run monthly automated evaluations to ensure citation verification recall stays above 95%.

items.label: Redact PII from synthesized outputs unless explicitly authorized by data subject consent records
items.description: Apply a post-synthesis PII detection and redaction step that removes or masks personal financial data (account numbers, SSNs, transaction histories) from outputs unless the session has a verified GDPR lawful basis record for that data subject.

sections.heading: Operational Risk, Incident Response & Regulatory Reporting
sections.description: Establish the operational controls, monitoring infrastructure, and incident response procedures required to detect, contain, and report failures of the Agentic RAG system under fintech regulatory obligations.

items.label: Define a kill switch procedure that halts all agent sessions within 60 seconds
items.description: Implement a centralized circuit breaker that can immediately suspend all active agent sessions, freeze retrieval pipelines, and revert to static fallback responses, with a documented activation procedure tested quarterly.

items.label: Map agent failure modes to specific regulatory reporting obligations
items.description: Create a failure mode registry that maps each identified failure scenario (e.g., hallucinated credit data, unauthorized PII retrieval, AML false negative) to its required regulatory notification timeline (e.g., GDPR 72-hour breach notification, SAR filing deadlines).

items.label: Implement real-time BSA/AML pattern monitoring on agent-retrieved transaction data
items.description: Deploy a parallel monitoring pipeline that analyzes transaction data retrieved by the agent for BSA/AML red flags (structuring, layering, unusual velocity) and automatically generates Suspicious Activity Report drafts for compliance officer review.

items.label: Conduct quarterly red team exercises simulating prompt injection against financial data sources
items.description: Engage an internal or third-party red team to attempt prompt injection attacks designed to manipulate the agent's query planner into retrieving unauthorized financial data or producing non-compliant outputs, with findings remediated within 30 days.

items.label: Establish SLAs for human-in-the-loop review of escalated agent outputs
items.description: Define and contractually enforce maximum review times for compliance officer escalations: 4 hours for credit decision outputs, 1 hour for outputs flagging potential AML activity, and 15 minutes for outputs involving suspected MNPI.

items.label: Integrate agent audit logs with your SIEM and compliance monitoring platform
items.description: Stream all agent query planning logs, retrieval audit trails, and output records to your SIEM (e.g., Splunk, IBM QRadar) with pre-built correlation rules that alert on SOX control failures, PCI DSS violations, and anomalous retrieval patterns.

items.label: Perform annual third-party model risk management review per SR 11-7 guidance
items.description: Commission an independent model risk management review of the Agentic RAG system annually, covering conceptual soundness, data quality, ongoing monitoring, and outcomes analysis, documented in a format satisfying Federal Reserve SR 11-7 requirements.

notes.note: All agent query plans, retrieved document chunks, synthesized outputs, and citation metadata must be retained in tamper-evident, WORM-compliant storage for a minimum of 7 years. Retrieval logs constitute books and records under SEC Rule 17a-4 if the agent's outputs are used in connection with securities transactions or investment advice, making immutable audit trails a non-negotiable architectural requirement.

Any retrieval corpus or vector index containing Primary Account Numbers (PANs), CVVs, or cardholder data must be treated as a Cardholder Data Environment (CDE). This requires AES-256 encryption at rest (Req. 3.5), TLS 1.3 in transit (Req. 4.2.1), strict access controls on the retrieval pipeline (Req. 7), and quarterly vulnerability scans of all infrastructure components that process or transmit retrieved payment data.

When the Agentic RAG system retrieves and synthesizes personal financial data of EU or California residents, Article 22 automated decision-making rights apply if outputs influence credit, insurance, or financial product eligibility. Implement a data subject rights workflow that can locate and purge a specific individual's data from all retrieval indexes within 30 days of a deletion request, and ensure the agent never synthesizes outputs based solely on automated processing without a documented lawful basis.

If the agent retrieves and analyzes transaction data as part of its workflow, the institution's BSA/AML program obligations extend to agent-generated outputs. Automated synthesis of transaction patterns that could constitute suspicious activity must be reviewed by a qualified BSA officer before any SAR filing decision is made. The agent must not be the sole decision-maker for SAR filing determinations, and all agent-assisted AML analyses must be documented in the institution's BSA compliance records with human sign-off.

cta.heading: Automate Your Fintech Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your Agentic RAG pipeline against this checklist—automatically detecting hallucinations, citation failures, and regulatory drift before they become enforcement actions.
